steps.title: Remove the irrelevant vertical shift
steps.content: The table gives points such as $(0,n)$ and $(3,n+14)$. The unknown $n$ adds the same vertical shift to the line, but slope only depends on how much $y$ changes when $x$ changes. Set $n=0$ to make the table numeric: $(0,0)$ and $(3,14)$.

steps.title: Read the slope
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$ in Desmos. The regression gives $m=\frac{14}{3}$, or about $4.667$. Algebraically this is $\frac{(n+14)-n}{3-0}=\frac{14}{3}$, so choice C is correct.
